dcterms:title: Arbin BTS XLSX — BDF column mapping
dcterms:description: Maps Arbin Battery Test System XLSX export column headers to BDF canonical columns. Arbin XLSX workbooks are multi-sheet: a 'Global_Info' metadata sheet, one or more 'Channel_*' time-series sheets (mapped here), an optional 'ACIM_chan_1' EIS sheet, and 'StatisticsByCycle_chan_1' / 'StatisticsByStep_chan_1' summary sheets. Column headers appear in both space-separated (Charge Capacity (Ah)) and underscore-separated (Charge_Capacity(Ah)) dialects depending on the MITS / MITS_PRO / DataPro software version. The Charge/Discharge Capacity and Energy accumulators reset at schedule-authored boundaries not recoverable from the time-series, so they map to the schedule-scoped BDF terms (schedule_charging_capacity_ah, etc.). For the full per-column vendor semantics, including vendor-only columns with no canonical equivalent and the EIS / statistics / metadata sheets, see the Arbin vendor ontology module at .../vendors/arbin.

csvw:columns.csvw:name: schedule_charging_capacity_ah
csvw:columns.skos:note: Arbin accumulator with schedule-authored reset windows; maps to the schedule-scoped BDF term rather than a cycle- or step-scoped one (the reset rule is not recoverable from the exported time-series).

csvw:columns.csvw:name: temperature_t1_celsius
csvw:columns.skos:note: Unqualified / numbered Arbin auxiliary temperature channel; maps to temperature_t1_celsius per the BDF numbered-channel convention. Additional channels (Aux_Temperature_2, _3, …) correspond to temperature_t2_celsius, temperature_t3_celsius, … — see the Arbin ontology module.

csvw:columns.csvw:name: unix_time_second
csvw:columns.skos:note: Datetime string (or Excel serial in some exports); converted to Unix epoch seconds by the BDF normaliser.
